qdc-price-list-doha-2026.question: What is QDC (Qatar Distribution Company) in Doha?
qdc-price-list-doha-2026.answer: QDC (Qatar Distribution Company) is the only government-licensed take-home alcohol retailer in Qatar. Located primarily in Abu Hamour, Doha, it sells beer, wine, and spirits exclusively to non-Muslim residents holding a valid QDC liquor permit. Tourists cannot purchase from QDC.

qdc-price-list-doha-2026.question: How much does alcohol cost at QDC in Qatar?
qdc-price-list-doha-2026.answer: At QDC in 2026, beer costs approximately QAR 4–6 per can or QAR 85–125 for a 24-pack. Wine ranges from QAR 35–200+ per bottle. Spirits like Johnnie Walker Red Label cost QAR 90–110 and premium vodkas like Grey Goose run QAR 150–190 for 700ml. Prices are government-regulated and higher than most duty-free options.

qdc-price-list-doha-2026.question: How do I get a QDC permit in Qatar?
qdc-price-list-doha-2026.answer: To get a QDC permit: visit a QDC store with your Qatar ID, prove non-Muslim status, provide a salary certificate showing QAR 3,000+ monthly income, and submit an employer NOC if required. The permit card is issued quickly and comes with a monthly purchase limit based on your salary.

qdc-price-list-doha-2026.question: Can tourists buy alcohol from QDC Qatar?
qdc-price-list-doha-2026.answer: No. Tourists cannot buy from QDC — it requires a resident permit. Tourists can legally drink alcohol at licensed hotel bars, restaurants, and clubs throughout Doha. Most major hotels have at least one licensed bar or restaurant.

qdc-price-list-doha-2026.question: Is QDC open during Ramadan?
qdc-price-list-doha-2026.answer: QDC has significantly reduced hours during Ramadan and may close on certain days. Contact the store directly or check current hours before visiting during the holy month. Hotel bars also operate with restricted hours after Iftar during Ramadan.

drinking-age-qatar-guide.question: What is the legal drinking age in Qatar?
drinking-age-qatar-guide.answer: The legal drinking age in Qatar is 21 years old. This applies to both tourists and residents at all licensed venues including hotel bars and restaurants. Qatar strictly enforces this age limit.

drinking-age-qatar-guide.question: Can you drink alcohol at 18 in Qatar?
drinking-age-qatar-guide.answer: No. The minimum drinking age in Qatar is 21, not 18. Anyone under 21 cannot legally consume alcohol at any venue in Qatar. Hotels and licensed venues check IDs and face penalties for serving underage customers.

drinking-age-qatar-guide.question: Where can tourists drink alcohol in Qatar?
drinking-age-qatar-guide.answer: Tourists over 21 can drink alcohol at licensed hotel bars, hotel restaurants, and licensed clubs in Qatar. Alcohol is not available in supermarkets or convenience stores. The QDC (Qatar Distribution Company) requires a resident permit and is not accessible to tourists.

drinking-age-qatar-guide.question: Is it legal to drink alcohol in public in Qatar?
drinking-age-qatar-guide.answer: No. Drinking alcohol in public is strictly illegal in Qatar for everyone. Alcohol consumption is only permitted inside licensed hotel venues. Public drinking can result in fines, arrest, or deportation for foreign nationals.

drinking-age-qatar-guide.question: What is the drinking age in Qatar for tourists?
drinking-age-qatar-guide.answer: The drinking age for tourists in Qatar is 21 years old, the same as for residents. There is no exception for tourists. Carry your passport as ID when visiting hotel bars in Qatar.

best-restaurants-doha-2026.question: What is the best restaurant in Doha, Qatar?
best-restaurants-doha-2026.answer: Doha's top restaurants include Idam by Alain Ducasse at the Museum of Islamic Art (French-Qatari fusion with stunning Corniche views), Nobu Doha at the Four Seasons (Japanese-Peruvian fusion), and Gordon Ramsay's Hell's Kitchen at Mondrian Doha. For authentic Qatari food, Al Aker at Souq Waqif is excellent.

best-restaurants-doha-2026.question: Where can I eat traditional Qatari food in Doha?
best-restaurants-doha-2026.answer: The best places for traditional Qatari food in Doha are Souq Waqif (especially Al Aker and Al Shurfa restaurants), Katara Cultural Village (Layali Zaman), and various local Qatari restaurants in residential neighborhoods. Try machboos (spiced rice with meat), harees, and saloona stew.

best-restaurants-doha-2026.question: Are there cheap restaurants in Doha?
best-restaurants-doha-2026.answer: Yes. Budget dining in Doha is excellent — Indian and Pakistani restaurants in Al Mansoura serve biryani for QAR 10–25. Shawarma stands cost QAR 5–15. Mall food courts have wide choice for QAR 20–50. Food delivery apps like Talabat offer great value deals.

best-restaurants-doha-2026.question: Do restaurants in Doha serve alcohol?
best-restaurants-doha-2026.answer: Only licensed hotel restaurants and hotel bars serve alcohol in Doha. The majority of restaurants — including those in malls, Souq Waqif, and standalone venues — are alcohol-free (halal). If alcohol is important to you, book specifically at hotel restaurants or licensed clubs.

best-restaurants-doha-2026.question: What food is Doha known for?
best-restaurants-doha-2026.answer: Doha and Qatar are known for dishes including machboos (spiced rice with slow-cooked lamb or chicken), harees (slow-cooked wheat and meat), saloona (spiced stew), majboos, and fresh seafood including hammour (grouper). Dates, Arabic coffee, and chai karak (spiced tea) are iconic Qatari drinks.

doha-shopping-guide-2026.question: What is the best mall in Doha, Qatar?
doha-shopping-guide-2026.answer: The best mall in Doha depends on your needs. Mall of Qatar is the largest with 500+ stores and great family entertainment. Villaggio is famous for its stunning Italian-themed architecture with an indoor canal. Place Vendôme is best for luxury brands. City Centre Doha is best for convenience with metro access.

doha-shopping-guide-2026.question: Where should I shop in Doha for souvenirs?
doha-shopping-guide-2026.answer: Souq Waqif is the best place for traditional souvenirs in Doha — spices, saffron, oud perfumes, Arabic crafts, prayer beads, and traditional clothing. Prices are negotiable. The nearby Gold Souq is great for gold jewellery at competitive prices.

doha-shopping-guide-2026.question: Is shopping tax-free in Qatar?
doha-shopping-guide-2026.answer: Qatar does not impose VAT (value added tax) on most retail goods, making shopping attractive compared to countries with 10–25% VAT. However, some specific items have duty. Overall, prices for international brands are similar to or slightly higher than in Europe/US due to import costs.

doha-shopping-guide-2026.question: What are the shopping mall opening hours in Doha?
doha-shopping-guide-2026.answer: Most Doha malls open from 10am to 10pm on Sunday through Wednesday, and extend to 11pm–midnight on Thursday, Friday, and Saturday (the Gulf weekend). During Ramadan, malls typically open in the afternoon and stay open late into the night after Iftar.

qatar-duty-free-guide-2026.question: What is available at Qatar Duty Free at Hamad Airport?
qatar-duty-free-guide-2026.answer: Qatar Duty Free at Hamad International Airport offers alcohol (spirits, wine, beer), perfumes and fragrances (international and Arabic oud), electronics (Apple, Sony), luxury goods (Rolex, Cartier, LV), dates and local food products, chocolates, and a wide range of souvenirs. Over 200 brands are represented.

qatar-duty-free-guide-2026.question: What are the alcohol prices at Qatar Airport duty free?
qatar-duty-free-guide-2026.answer: Approximate Qatar Duty Free alcohol prices in 2026: Johnnie Walker Black Label 1L around USD 30–35, Grey Goose Vodka 1L around USD 35–40, wine per bottle from USD 12–60+, Champagne (Moët) around USD 40–50. These are among the best alcohol prices available to passengers transiting through Doha.

qatar-duty-free-guide-2026.question: Can I bring alcohol from Qatar Duty Free into Qatar?
qatar-duty-free-guide-2026.answer: No. You cannot bring duty-free alcohol purchased at the departures hall into Qatar. It is confiscated at customs. Duty-free alcohol at HIA is only for consumption during your international journey outside Qatar. The QDC (for residents) is the only way to legally bring alcohol home in Qatar.

qatar-duty-free-guide-2026.question: Is Qatar Duty Free open for transit passengers?
qatar-duty-free-guide-2026.answer: Yes. Qatar Duty Free is fully accessible to transit passengers during their layover at Hamad International Airport. The duty-free area is open 24/7. Transit passengers can shop for alcohol, perfumes, electronics, and luxury goods during their layover.

doha-nightlife-guide-2026.question: Is there nightlife in Doha, Qatar?
doha-nightlife-guide-2026.answer: Yes. Doha has a growing nightlife scene including world-class hotel rooftop bars (Four Seasons Sky View, Iris Doha at The Pearl), DJ nightclubs in major hotels, Arabic shisha cafes at Souq Waqif open until midnight, cultural evening events at Katara, and beautiful evening promenades along the Corniche and The Pearl marina.

doha-nightlife-guide-2026.question: What are the best bars in Doha?
doha-nightlife-guide-2026.answer: The best bars in Doha include: Sky View Bar at the Four Seasons (panoramic Corniche views), Iris Doha at The Pearl (outdoor terrace with marina views), Wahm Bar at the W Doha (stylish cocktail bar), and Spire Bar at InterContinental (great city views). All require guests to be 21+.

doha-nightlife-guide-2026.question: Are there clubs in Doha?
doha-nightlife-guide-2026.answer: Yes. Doha has hotel-based nightclubs including Qube Club at Crowne Plaza, Carbon Club, and Groove at Marriott. Clubs open late (midnight+) and peak on Thursday and Friday nights. Entry is usually free before midnight; cover charges may apply later.

doha-nightlife-guide-2026.question: How much do drinks cost at bars in Doha?
doha-nightlife-guide-2026.answer: Hotel bar drinks in Doha typically cost QAR 50–150 for cocktails, QAR 40–80 for beer, and QAR 60–120 for a glass of wine. Prices are high compared to Europe or the US due to government taxes on licensed venues. Budget QAR 200–400+ per person for a night out at hotel bars.

alcohol-guide-doha.question: Can tourists drink alcohol in Qatar?
alcohol-guide-doha.answer: Yes. Tourists can drink alcohol at licensed hotel bars, restaurants, and clubs in Qatar. You do not need a permit — only residents do. Alcohol is not available in supermarkets; it is sold only at designated hotel venues and the QDC (Qatar Distribution Company) for permit holders.

alcohol-guide-doha.question: What is the drinking age in Qatar?
alcohol-guide-doha.answer: The legal drinking age in Qatar is 21 years old. This applies to both tourists and residents. Hotels and bars will check ID for anyone who looks underage.

alcohol-guide-doha.question: What is the QDC and how do I get a permit?
alcohol-guide-doha.answer: QDC (Qatar Distribution Company) is the government-licensed retailer for take-home alcohol purchases. To get a QDC permit, you must be a non-Muslim resident of Qatar, have a QID (Qatar ID), earn above a minimum salary (typically QAR 3,000+), and apply in person at a QDC store. Monthly purchase limits apply based on your salary.

alcohol-guide-doha.question: Can I bring alcohol into Qatar in my luggage?
alcohol-guide-doha.answer: No. Personal importation of alcohol into Qatar is completely prohibited. Any alcohol in your luggage when arriving at Hamad International Airport will be confiscated. You cannot bring duty-free alcohol from other countries into Qatar.

alcohol-guide-doha.question: Is alcohol allowed during Ramadan in Qatar?
alcohol-guide-doha.answer: Yes, but with significant restrictions during Ramadan. Hotel bars remain open but usually only after Iftar (sunset) and with reduced hours. Public drinking is completely prohibited. Non-Muslims are expected to be discreet. QDC stores operate with restricted hours during Ramadan.

alcohol-guide-doha.question: Where can I buy alcohol in Doha?
alcohol-guide-doha.answer: Alcohol in Doha can be purchased at licensed hotel bars and restaurants (open to everyone), and at QDC stores (Qatar Distribution Company) for residents with a valid permit. There are no supermarket alcohol sales in Qatar.

women-safety-dress-code-doha-qatar.question: Is Doha safe for solo female travelers?
women-safety-dress-code-doha-qatar.answer: Yes, Doha is extremely safe for solo female travelers. Qatar has one of the lowest crime rates in the world. Women can walk around freely, use public transport, and explore the city alone day or night. Street harassment is very uncommon and culturally unacceptable.

women-safety-dress-code-doha-qatar.question: What is the dress code for women in Qatar?
women-safety-dress-code-doha-qatar.answer: Women in Qatar should dress modestly in public areas — covering shoulders and knees is recommended. Swimwear is acceptable at hotel pools and beaches. There is no law requiring non-Muslim women to wear a hijab. Inside malls, restaurants, and attractions, smart casual clothing is fine. In mosques, full modest covering including a headscarf is required.

women-safety-dress-code-doha-qatar.question: Can women drive in Qatar?
women-safety-dress-code-doha-qatar.answer: Yes, women can freely drive in Qatar. There are no restrictions on women driving. Women can rent cars, hold Qatar driving licenses, and drive anywhere. Qatar has had no restrictions on women driving historically.

women-safety-dress-code-doha-qatar.question: Can unmarried women travel to Qatar?
women-safety-dress-code-doha-qatar.answer: Yes, unmarried women can travel to Qatar freely. There is no requirement to travel with a male guardian (mahram). You do not need to be married to visit or live in Qatar. Hotels accommodate single female guests without any issues.

women-safety-dress-code-doha-qatar.question: What should female tourists wear in Doha malls?
women-safety-dress-code-doha-qatar.answer: Smart casual clothing is perfectly acceptable in Doha malls — jeans, t-shirts, and dresses above the knee are commonly worn without issue. Very revealing clothing (extremely short skirts, very low-cut tops) may attract stares. Most major malls have signs requesting modest dress out of cultural respect.

cost-of-living-doha-2025.question: How much does it cost to live in Doha per month?
cost-of-living-doha-2025.answer: A single expat in Doha typically needs QAR 12,000–20,000 per month (approximately $3,300–$5,500 USD) for a comfortable lifestyle including rent, food, transport, and leisure. Families with children in international schools need QAR 35,000–60,000+ per month due to high school fees (QAR 30,000–120,000 per year per child).

cost-of-living-doha-2025.question: Is Qatar expensive to live in?
cost-of-living-doha-2025.answer: Qatar is relatively expensive compared to most countries, particularly for housing and international school fees. However, Qatar has zero personal income tax, meaning your gross salary is your take-home pay. Many expats find they save more in Qatar than in their home countries despite higher living costs.

cost-of-living-doha-2025.question: How much is rent in Doha in 2026?
cost-of-living-doha-2025.answer: Rent in Doha in 2026 ranges from QAR 4,000–7,000/month for a 1-bedroom apartment and QAR 7,000–14,000/month for a 2-bedroom. Luxury compounds and West Bay can cost QAR 10,000–20,000+ for a 2-bedroom. Prices have risen significantly since the 2022 World Cup.

cost-of-living-doha-2025.question: Do expats pay income tax in Qatar?
cost-of-living-doha-2025.answer: No. Qatar has no personal income tax. All salary earned in Qatar is completely tax-free regardless of nationality. There is also no capital gains tax, wealth tax, or inheritance tax for individuals in Qatar.

cost-of-living-doha-2025.question: Is food expensive in Doha?
cost-of-living-doha-2025.answer: Food costs in Doha are moderate to high. Eating at a local restaurant costs QAR 25–60 per meal. International restaurant dining runs QAR 80–200+ per person. Grocery shopping for home cooking at Lulu Hypermarket costs QAR 500–1,000/month for one person. Imported items (cheese, wine-free alcohol substitutes, branded goods) are more expensive.

doha-safety-guide-tourists-expats.question: Is Doha safe for tourists?
doha-safety-guide-tourists-expats.answer: Yes, Doha is one of the safest cities in the world for tourists. Qatar consistently ranks in the global top 10 for safety, with extremely low crime rates, a strong police presence, and a culture that is very respectful of visitors. Violent crime against tourists is virtually unheard of.

doha-safety-guide-tourists-expats.question: Is Doha safe for solo female travelers?
doha-safety-guide-tourists-expats.answer: Yes. Doha is widely considered very safe for solo female travelers. Street harassment is uncommon and socially unacceptable. Women can move around freely, use taxis and the metro, eat alone in restaurants, and explore the city independently. Modest dress (covering shoulders and knees in public) is recommended but not strictly enforced for tourists.

doha-safety-guide-tourists-expats.question: What are the emergency numbers in Qatar?
doha-safety-guide-tourists-expats.answer: In Qatar, dial 999 for police, 998 for ambulance and medical emergencies, and 997 for fire. The tourist police hotline is +974 4444 1744. All emergency services are available 24/7.

doha-safety-guide-tourists-expats.question: Is it safe to walk around Doha at night?
doha-safety-guide-tourists-expats.answer: Yes, Doha is safe to walk at night. The Corniche, Souq Waqif, The Pearl, and Katara are all popular evening destinations that are well-lit and patrolled. The streets are very safe after dark by global standards.

doha-safety-guide-tourists-expats.question: What should I not do in Qatar as a tourist?
doha-safety-guide-tourists-expats.answer: Tourists should avoid: public displays of affection, drinking alcohol in non-licensed venues, wearing revealing clothing in public areas (malls, markets), photographing government buildings or people without permission, and importing pork or alcohol. Respecting local customs and Islamic culture ensures a trouble-free visit.

rooftop-bars-doha-2026.question: Are there rooftop bars in Doha, Qatar?
rooftop-bars-doha-2026.answer: Yes. Doha has a strong rooftop bar scene within its five-star hotels. Top options include Sky View Bar at the Four Seasons, Iris Doha at The Pearl, WAHM at the W Doha, Horizon at the St. Regis, and Alto at the Andaz.

rooftop-bars-doha-2026.question: Which rooftop bar has the best view in Doha?
rooftop-bars-doha-2026.answer: Sky View Bar at the Four Seasons Doha is consistently rated as having the best view — panoramic West Bay skyline and Corniche from a high floor. Horizon at the St. Regis and Iris at The Pearl also have outstanding marina and Gulf views.

rooftop-bars-doha-2026.question: What is the dress code at rooftop bars in Doha?
rooftop-bars-doha-2026.answer: Smart casual is the standard dress code: no shorts, no flip-flops, no sportswear. On Thursday and Friday nights at venues like Iris and WAHM, the expected standard is closer to smart-stylish. Men: clean dark jeans or trousers plus a collared shirt. Women: resort-smart or cocktail attire.

rooftop-bars-doha-2026.question: Is there happy hour at bars in Doha?
rooftop-bars-doha-2026.answer: Yes. Most hotel bars in Doha run happy hour promotions from roughly 5–8pm, offering 2-for-1 cocktails or discounted drinks. Iris Doha and W Doha WAHM are particularly known for competitive early-evening deals. Check each venue's current offers on Instagram before visiting.

rooftop-bars-doha-2026.question: Which rooftop bar is best for couples in Doha?
rooftop-bars-doha-2026.answer: Horizon Lounge at the St. Regis Doha is the best choice for couples seeking a romantic, intimate rooftop experience with exceptional cocktails and champagne. For a more lively, vibrant setting, Iris Doha at The Pearl combines a beautiful marina view with great energy.

free-things-to-do-doha-2026.question: What is free to do in Doha, Qatar?
free-things-to-do-doha-2026.answer: Doha has many free activities: the 7km Corniche promenade, MIA Park, Katara Beach, Aspire Park, Al Bidda Park, wandering Souq Waqif, the Pearl marina walk, Qatar National Library, Fire Station galleries, and regular free events at Katara Cultural Village.

free-things-to-do-doha-2026.question: Are museums free in Doha?
free-things-to-do-doha-2026.answer: Some are. The Qatar National Library and Fire Station galleries are always free. Certain Msheireb museums offer free entry on specific days. The Museum of Islamic Art and National Museum of Qatar charge modest fees (QAR 30–50). Many Katara Cultural Village gallery spaces are free.

free-things-to-do-doha-2026.question: Can tourists access the beach in Doha for free?
free-things-to-do-doha-2026.answer: Yes. Katara Beach within Katara Cultural Village is free to access and is the most popular free public beach. Al Wakra public beach is also free. Some hotel beaches charge day-pass fees (QAR 100–400), but these are optional — public beaches are genuinely free and well-maintained.

free-things-to-do-doha-2026.question: Is Doha expensive for tourists?
free-things-to-do-doha-2026.answer: Doha can be expensive if you focus on hotels and restaurants, but many of the city's best experiences are free — the Corniche, parks, souqs, public beaches, and cultural spaces. Budget travellers can have a full, rewarding day in Doha spending very little beyond food costs.

free-things-to-do-doha-2026.question: Is the Corniche in Doha free to walk?
free-things-to-do-doha-2026.answer: Yes, completely free. The 7km Corniche promenade is a public space open 24 hours, with no entry fee, excellent lighting at night, and stunning views of the West Bay skyline and the Arabian Gulf.

doha-family-activities-2026.question: Is Doha family-friendly for tourists?
doha-family-activities-2026.answer: Yes. Doha is one of the most family-friendly cities in the Gulf. It is very safe, has excellent public infrastructure, plenty of air-conditioned indoor attractions, free parks, beaches, and a wide range of child-focused activities from theme parks (Angry Birds World, KidzMondo) to desert safaris and cultural village experiences.

doha-family-activities-2026.question: What indoor activities are available for kids in Doha?
doha-family-activities-2026.answer: Excellent indoor options for kids in Doha include Angry Birds World at Doha Festival City (world's largest Angry Birds theme park), KidzMondo at Villaggio Mall (interactive mini city for ages 2–14), the Villaggio Mall gondola rides and ice rink, and various play centres within major malls.

doha-family-activities-2026.question: What is the best age for a desert safari in Qatar?
doha-family-activities-2026.answer: Children aged 5 and above are generally comfortable with desert dune bashing. Many operators recommend a minimum age of 4–5 for active dune activities, but younger children can attend and enjoy the beach arrival at the Inland Sea (Khor Al Adaid). Always confirm age/weight minimums with your chosen safari operator.

doha-family-activities-2026.question: Is Doha Zoo worth visiting with kids?
doha-family-activities-2026.answer: Yes, particularly for younger children (under 10). Doha Zoo houses 1,500+ animals and has been well maintained. It's most enjoyable in the cooler months (October–April). Entry fees are low at QAR 10–30 per person. For a half-day outing, it's a good value family activity.

qdc-store-locations-doha-2026.question: Where is QDC (Qatar Distribution Company) in Doha?
qdc-store-locations-doha-2026.answer: The main QDC store is in Abu Hamour, off Industrial Area Road near Salwa Road. There is also a Salwa Road branch. Both require a valid QDC permit card. For branches outside Doha, QDC operates in Al Khor, Ras Laffan, and Um Alhoul (Mesaieed).

qdc-store-locations-doha-2026.question: How many QDC branches are there in Qatar?
qdc-store-locations-doha-2026.answer: Qatar has approximately 5 QDC branches as of 2026: Abu Hamour (main store), Salwa Road, Al Khor, Ras Laffan, and Um Alhoul (Mesaieed). The Abu Hamour store is the largest and carries the widest product range.

qdc-store-locations-doha-2026.question: What are QDC store opening hours?
qdc-store-locations-doha-2026.answer: All QDC branches open Monday–Thursday and Saturday–Sunday from 9:00 AM to 9:00 PM. On Fridays, hours are reduced to 2:00 PM – 9:00 PM due to Friday prayers. All branches are closed on Qatar public holidays and operate reduced hours during Ramadan.

qdc-store-locations-doha-2026.question: Is QDC open on Friday?
qdc-store-locations-doha-2026.answer: Yes, QDC stores are open on Fridays but with reduced hours: 2:00 PM – 9:00 PM. Friday morning is closed due to the Friday prayer congregation. This applies to all QDC branches across Qatar.

qdc-store-locations-doha-2026.question: Does QDC offer delivery?
qdc-store-locations-doha-2026.answer: QDC has explored delivery options, but as of 2026 purchases are primarily made in-store. Check the QDC official website or app for current delivery availability. All purchases, whether in-store or delivered, count toward your monthly permit allowance.

doha-beach-guide-2026.question: What is the best public beach in Doha?
doha-beach-guide-2026.answer: Katara Beach within Katara Cultural Village is the best free public beach in Doha — clean, well-maintained, calm water, and centrally located. Al Wakra Beach (25km south) is a great alternative for more space and fewer crowds.

doha-beach-guide-2026.question: Can tourists use hotel beaches in Doha?
doha-beach-guide-2026.answer: Yes. Most five-star hotels in Doha offer day-pass beach club access to non-staying guests. Day passes typically cost QAR 150–400 per person and often include food and beverage credit. The best hotel beach clubs include Sharq Village (Ritz-Carlton), Marriott Marquis, and InterContinental Doha.

doha-beach-guide-2026.question: Is it safe to swim at beaches in Doha?
doha-beach-guide-2026.answer: Yes, swimming is generally safe in the Arabian Gulf around Doha. The water is calm with no surf. Katara Beach, Al Wakra, and Sealine all have calm, swimmable water. Jellyfish can occasionally appear in autumn — check locally. The main hazard is sun intensity; always use strong sunscreen.

doha-beach-guide-2026.question: Are bikinis allowed on beaches in Doha?
doha-beach-guide-2026.answer: Bikinis are appropriate at hotel beach clubs where western swimwear norms apply. At public beaches like Katara and Al Wakra, modest swimwear is recommended (one-piece, bikini with cover-up, or board shorts). There is no strict law against bikinis on public beaches, but cultural sensitivity is appreciated.

qatar-public-holidays-2026.question: How many public holidays does Qatar have in 2026?
qatar-public-holidays-2026.answer: Qatar has approximately 7 official public holidays in 2026: New Year's Day (Jan 1), National Sports Day (Feb 10), Eid Al Fitr (~3 days in late March), Eid Al Adha (~3 days in early June), Islamic New Year (~June 26), Prophet's Birthday (~September 4), and National Day (Dec 18). Islamic holiday dates are confirmed by moon sighting.

qatar-public-holidays-2026.question: Is Eid a public holiday in Qatar?
qatar-public-holidays-2026.answer: Yes. Both Eid Al Fitr (end of Ramadan, ~March 2026) and Eid Al Adha (Feast of Sacrifice, ~June 2026) are official public holidays in Qatar. Each is typically 3 days for public sector workers. Many private employers extend this to 4–5 days.

qatar-public-holidays-2026.question: When is Qatar National Day?
qatar-public-holidays-2026.answer: Qatar National Day is always on December 18. It celebrates the 1878 unification of Qatar under Sheikh Jassim bin Mohammed Al Thani. It is Qatar's biggest national celebration, featuring a military parade on the Corniche, fireworks, heritage events, and public concerts.

qatar-public-holidays-2026.question: Do private companies in Qatar close on public holidays?
qatar-public-holidays-2026.answer: Qatar labour law requires private sector employers to grant the official public holidays. In practice, most established companies observe them. Hospitality, retail, and some industries may remain operational with adjusted hours, offering compensatory leave or pay for employees who work on public holidays.

doha-weather-monthly-guide-2026.question: What is the best month to visit Doha?
doha-weather-monthly-guide-2026.answer: January is the best single month — temperatures of 18–23°C, low humidity, and perfect conditions for outdoor sightseeing, beach visits, and desert trips. The entire window from November through March is excellent. October is an underrated shoulder-season option with decent weather and lower hotel prices.

doha-weather-monthly-guide-2026.question: What is the hottest month in Doha?
doha-weather-monthly-guide-2026.answer: July is the hottest month in Doha, with average highs of 40–45°C. Combined with Arabian Gulf humidity, heat index values can reach 55–60°C. June, July, and August are all extreme summer months and are not recommended for outdoor tourism.

doha-weather-monthly-guide-2026.question: Does it rain in Doha?
doha-weather-monthly-guide-2026.answer: Very rarely. Doha averages only 70–80mm of rainfall per year. Brief showers can occur between November and March. It almost never rains in summer. When rain does fall in winter, it can be heavy but short-lived.

doha-weather-monthly-guide-2026.question: What should I wear in Doha?
doha-weather-monthly-guide-2026.answer: In winter (Nov–Mar): light cottons and linens during the day, a light jacket or layer for evenings. In spring (Apr–May): light, loose, breathable clothing. In summer (Jun–Sep): the lightest possible clothing outdoors; air-conditioned interiors are intensely cold so carry a layer. Always dress modestly (cover shoulders and knees) in religious sites and traditional areas.

doha-expat-guide-2026.question: Is Doha a good place to live for expats?
doha-expat-guide-2026.answer: For the right person and job, yes. Doha offers tax-free income, exceptional safety, good healthcare and international schools, a cosmopolitan lifestyle, and easy access to beach and desert. The main challenges are extreme summer heat, cultural conservatism in public spaces, and distance from home countries.

doha-expat-guide-2026.question: Is English widely spoken in Doha?
doha-expat-guide-2026.answer: Yes. English is the de facto language of business, commerce, and expat life in Doha. Street signs are bilingual (Arabic/English), most customer-facing staff speak English, and multinational workplaces operate primarily in English. Arabic is useful but not required for most expat daily life.

doha-expat-guide-2026.question: How is healthcare quality in Qatar?
doha-expat-guide-2026.answer: Excellent. Qatar has invested heavily in healthcare infrastructure. Hamad Medical Corporation runs the main public hospitals including the world-class Sidra Medicine. Private hospitals (Aster, Al Ahli, specialized clinics) provide fast, high-quality care with minimal wait times for insured patients. Most conditions can be treated in Qatar without medical tourism.

doha-expat-guide-2026.question: How long does it take to settle into life in Doha?
doha-expat-guide-2026.answer: Most expats feel comfortable in Doha after 3–6 months. The first month involves bureaucratic setup (QID, bank account, school registration, driving licence), the second and third months involve building social connections and discovering the city. By six months, most expats have established routines and a social circle.

doha-expat-guide-2026.question: What are the best neighbourhoods in Doha for expats?
doha-expat-guide-2026.answer: The Pearl for luxury walkable living; West Bay for CBD proximity; Al Waab for family-friendly villa life near schools; Lusail for modern apartments and growing community; Madinat Khalifa for established mid-range residential living. The right choice depends on budget, family situation, and workplace location.

doha-at-night-guide-2026.question: What is open at night in Doha?
doha-at-night-guide-2026.answer: Doha has a vibrant night scene: Souq Waqif is lively until midnight+ (weekends especially), hotel bars run until 1–2am, malls open until 11pm–midnight, the Pearl marina is active on Thursday and Friday evenings, and dhow cruises depart evenings from the Corniche harbour. Evening Corniche walks are popular any night of the week.

doha-at-night-guide-2026.question: Where are the best shisha cafes in Doha?
doha-at-night-guide-2026.answer: The best shisha is at Souq Waqif, where dozens of open-air cafe terraces serve flavoured shisha (QAR 50–100) alongside karak tea and fresh juices in the atmospheric old market setting. For a premium experience, the shisha terraces at St. Regis and Four Seasons offer hotel-quality service with cocktails alongside (QAR 100–200 per shisha).

doha-at-night-guide-2026.question: Is it safe to walk at night in Doha?
doha-at-night-guide-2026.answer: Very safe. Qatar has one of the world's lowest crime rates and virtually no street crime targeting tourists. The Corniche, Souq Waqif, The Pearl, and Katara are all completely safe to walk at night. Women can walk alone safely in these areas. Standard urban awareness applies, but Doha is genuinely one of the safest cities for nighttime outdoor activity.

doha-at-night-guide-2026.question: What is the best area to go out at night in Doha?
doha-at-night-guide-2026.answer: Souq Waqif is the best area for an atmospheric, culturally rich evening — shisha, Arabic food, live music, and unique architecture. The Pearl is best for a modern cosmopolitan evening out with waterfront restaurants and cafes. For licensed bars and clubs, West Bay hotel district has the greatest concentration of options.

ramadan-in-doha-guide-2026.question: Can tourists eat during Ramadan in Qatar?
ramadan-in-doha-guide-2026.answer: Tourists can eat freely in private spaces: hotel rooms, hotel restaurants (which screen off eating areas during daytime), and private offices. Eating, drinking, or smoking in public during daylight Ramadan hours is prohibited by law for everyone in Qatar — tourist or resident. The rule is generally enforced politely, but should be respected.

ramadan-in-doha-guide-2026.question: What is Iftar and where can I experience it in Doha?
ramadan-in-doha-guide-2026.answer: Iftar is the meal that breaks the daily Ramadan fast at sunset. In Doha, hotels set up elaborate Ramadan tents offering lavish Iftar buffets (QAR 150–400 per person). Restaurants across the city also offer Iftar menus. The best hotel Ramadan tent experiences (Four Seasons, St. Regis, InterContinental) should be booked well in advance.

ramadan-in-doha-guide-2026.question: Do restaurants close during Ramadan in Doha?
ramadan-in-doha-guide-2026.answer: Most restaurants either close or operate with screened, limited service during daylight hours. After sunset at Iftar time, all restaurants open simultaneously and many operate until very late (2–3am or beyond). This creates a vibrant after-dark restaurant and cafe scene during Ramadan evenings that is more lively than normal non-Ramadan nights.

ramadan-in-doha-guide-2026.question: Which hotel offers the best Iftar buffet in Doha?
ramadan-in-doha-guide-2026.answer: The Four Seasons Doha, St. Regis Doha, and InterContinental Doha are consistently rated among the best Ramadan tent Iftar experiences, with elaborate themed settings, live Arabic music, and extensive Gulf-cuisine buffets. Prices range from QAR 250–400 per person. Reservations are essential — book at least a week in advance.

ramadan-in-doha-guide-2026.question: When is Ramadan 2026 in Qatar?
ramadan-in-doha-guide-2026.answer: Ramadan 2026 is expected to begin approximately February 17–18, 2026 (subject to official moon sighting confirmation by Qatar's religious authorities). It lasts 29 or 30 days, ending with Eid Al Fitr around March 28–30, 2026. The exact dates are announced officially 1–2 days before the start when the crescent moon is sighted.
